chunk_summary: Post-discharge nutrition for preterm and LBW infants focuses on tailored feeding strategies, supplementation, and growth monitoring to prevent deficiencies and ensure catch-up growth.
learning_objectives: Understand optimal feeding strategies for preterm and LBW infants post-discharge, including breastmilk fortification and formula use.
Learn supplementation guidelines for vitamin D, iron, calcium, and phosphorus in LBW and VLBW infants.
Recognize growth monitoring protocols using corrected age and appropriate growth charts.
key_facts: Vitamin D: 400 IU/day for LBW (1500–2499 g) and 800 IU/day for VLBW (<1500 g) until 6 months.
Iron: 2–4 mg/kg/day from 2 weeks in VLBW infants and 2–3 mg/kg/day from 6–8 weeks in LBW infants on human milk, continued to 6 months.
Calcium and phosphorus supplementation is often needed for VLBW infants on human milk until term-equivalent age.
Use enriched post-discharge formula (22–24 kcal/oz) if weight gain is insufficient despite optimized feeding techniques.
Monitor growth using corrected age with the Fenton chart initially, transitioning to WHO standards later.
reference_quote: Monitor growth using corrected age and an appropriate preterm growth chart (for example, the Fenton chart early, then transition to WHO standards).
document_key_topics: Post-discharge feeding strategies for preterm and LBW infants
Growth monitoring using corrected age and growth charts
Preventing growth faltering and metabolic bone disease
